🚀 SERIE: Camino a la Libertad Financiera

4/7 – Cómo elegir las MEJORES inversiones pasivas en Argentina 2026 (sin humo, solo opciones reales)

Con la desinflación en marcha (inflación ~2,2% mensual en marzo) y rendimientos altos, las inversiones pasivas son clave para construir tu número de libertad sin estrés. 

Olvidate de esquemas piramidales: acá van las reglas claras 👇

🔑 Las 3 reglas para elegir inversiones pasivas TOP:

1️⃣ Priorizá bajo riesgo y liquidez

• Empezá con FCI conservadores (IOL, Balanz con bonos soberanos UVA).
• Rendimiento real: ~3–5% mensual por encima de inflación.
• Liquidez: podés retirar en 24–48 hs si necesitás.

2️⃣ Buscá diversificación automática

• Fondos mixtos que incluyan dólar, plazos fijos y un toque de acciones (ej: YPF o Vista por el boom de Vaca Muerta).
• Apps como Mercado Pago o Ualá ofrecen opciones listas con comisiones <1%.

3️⃣ Chequeá rendimientos históricos y proyecciones

• Mirá brokers con fondos que rindieron +30% anual neto en 2025 (vs inflación ~35%).
• Para 2026, con economía creciendo 4%, apuntá a fondos con CEDEARs de dividendos (Apple, Coca‑Cola adaptados a Argentina).

📌 Con estas reglas, tu dinero trabaja solo y vos ganás tranquilidad.

Mercado Pago isn’t just another feature
It’s how $MELI wedges itself between you and your money. And then… it doesn’t move

Here is the deep dive

HOW IT REALLY WORKS 

Picture a restaurant:
You’re the customer
The merchant is the restaurant owner
The bank is the slow waiter who keeps messing up your order
Mercado Pago is: cash register + POS + account + credit + customer support (with AI) in one app

So Mercado Pago lets you keep money in the app and drives adoption by offering attractive yields on deposited funds (like a classic bank), so people leave liquidity there instead of letting it rot
They say AUM grew from $2B to almost $19B in three years, and Fintech MAU reached 78M in FY25.

Why is that powerful? Because when money lives there, every payment becomes a tap, not a drama

PAYMENT RAILS (TPV = how much fuel runs through the pipes)
MELI defines TPV as the total dollars paid using Mercado Pago (marketplace + off marketplace), excluding P2P.

The numbers:
FY25 TPV $277.8B,
Q4’25 $83.7B (+42% YoY; +52.6% FX neutral)

This is the master KPI: more TPV = more habit = more data = more products you can sell.

ACQUIRING (the Stripe/Adyen part, LatAm style)

This is where $MELI stops being just ecommerce and becomes infrastructure for merchants

Acquiring TPV = transactions settled using Mercado Pago’s processing/settlement services in marketplace and non marketplace, including POS, marketplace commerce, online checkout/link, and QR code.

FY25 Acquiring TPV: $188.1B.

To be more concrere if a store accepts payments with them, MELI enters the merchant’s daily cashflow.
And that’s sticky like glue.

CHECKOUT TOOLS (how they hook you online)

Fintech MAU also includes users making off platform payments using checkout or a payment link while logged into Mercado Pago.
So it’s not only I pay on MercadoLibre.
It’s “I pay anywhere and Mercado Pago is the button"
Huge difference my friend.

CREDIT (where the magic turns into monetization)

This is where you go from I move money to I price risk.

Credit portfolio: $12.5B in Q4’25 (+90% YoY)
NIMAL (spread after losses): 23.3% in Q4’25
Credit card 15–90 NPL: 4.4% (historic low)

And the gangster part (in a good way): they use ecosystem data (buyers + sellers + payments) to pre-approve and distribute credit right at the moment of conversion.

AI SUPPORT (lower cost to serve, higher loyalty)
Mercado Pago AI Assistant: in Q4’25 it handled 9M+ conversations, with ~90% resolved without a human.
In banking this is called: fixed costs that kill you,
Here it’s called: margin that can breathe

THE TAKE OF A GOOD INVESTOR 

Commerce is customer acquisition.
Fintech is principality (becoming the primary relationship) and the multi product monetization engine.
When wallet + acquiring + credit + UX come together, you’re not selling payments, you’re building a financial nervous system.
And $MELI is doing that in a market with huge TAM
